title: Forget Me Now

jokes.text: Why should you go to jail for a crime someone else noticed?
jokes.setup: Legal commercial promising to protect clients from prosecution
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical legal advice that sounds professional but means nothing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist legal speak that sounds important but is meaningless gibberish. Perfect parody of lawyer advertising.

jokes.text: Bob Loblaw, no habla Espanol
jokes.setup: Lawyer introducing himself
jokes.punchline: Name creates perfect rhyming phrase with Spanish disclaimer
jokes.explanation: Masterful wordplay that creates a perfect rhyming jingle. The name was clearly created for this specific pun, showing exceptional craft.

jokes.text: Well, we're not here to talk nonsense to Bob Loblaw
jokes.setup: Michael responding to the Spanish phrase
jokes.punchline: Accidentally creates another rhyming phrase while trying to be serious
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that extends the wordplay. Michael's unaware participation makes it character-specific.

jokes.text: How would you like someone going after some stupid person in your family? Now back off, you (bleep) ponce!
jokes.setup: George Sr. speaking through surrogate about being threatened
jokes.punchline: Aggressive British insult delivered by mild-mannered surrogate
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Sr.'s personality through proxy, though not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's my father. That's why we had the meeting here-- so that he couldn't interfere.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why they met away from his father
jokes.punchline: Father immediately interferes through surrogate
jokes.explanation: Solid irony showing the futility of trying to escape family dysfunction.

jokes.text: Interfere? I ought to pull down your pants and spank your ass raw.
jokes.setup: Professional legal meeting
jokes.punchline: Wildly inappropriate threat delivered by mild surrogate
jokes.explanation: Escalates the surrogate concept with inappropriate language, though relies on shock value.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. Have we met?
jokes.setup: Lawyer witnessing aggressive outburst
jokes.punchline: Polite, professional response to completely inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction that highlights the absurdity. Economy and structure are excellent.

jokes.text: Do you think he got that-- the two meanings?
jokes.setup: Bob Loblaw running away quickly after Lucille's sexual advance
jokes.punchline: Lucille proud of her own double entendre about being his 'partner'
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor where character analyzes her own joke. Very Lucille to be proud of her wordplay.

jokes.text: He's running pretty fast. I think he did.
jokes.setup: Lucille wondering if lawyer understood her sexual proposition
jokes.punchline: Michael's matter-of-fact observation that the lawyer's speed indicates understanding
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that plays off visual comedy of lawyer fleeing.

jokes.text: Let's call that a half hour. So you're not the only one who's got a date later.
jokes.setup: Professional legal consultation
jokes.punchline: Lawyer cutting session short to pursue Lucille romantically
jokes.explanation: Shows lawyer's unprofessionalism but is fairly predictable given Lucille's advance.

jokes.text: Oh, lunch? Well, better bring some dog food. 'Cause all the girls you date are dogs, and... Dogs love dog food, right, Pop?
jokes.setup: Michael mentioning lunch date
jokes.punchline: Gob's elaborate insult that overexplains itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - overexplaining a basic insult shows his desperation for approval. Character integration saves weak structure.

jokes.text: I was trying to make you laugh, Dad. That's all I ever wanted.
jokes.setup: Gob's failed insult being ignored by George Sr.
jokes.punchline: Pathetic admission of desperate need for father's approval
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment that's both funny and sad. Perfectly captures Gob's psychology.

jokes.text: Rita, don't forget your hat.
jokes.setup: Rita being dropped off for date with Michael
jokes.punchline: Reminder about hat suggests Rita has some kind of condition requiring head protection
jokes.explanation: Subtle foreshadowing that gains meaning on rewatch. Economy is perfect - just enough to plant the seed.

jokes.text: The world's first analrapist.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining his professional credentials
jokes.punchline: Unfortunate portmanteau that sounds like 'anal rapist'
jokes.explanation: Legendary AD joke. Perfect character integration - only Tobias would be this oblivious. Became cultural touchstone.

jokes.text: Yes, and you were almost arrested for those business cards.
jokes.setup: Tobias proud of his 'analrapist' credentials
jokes.punchline: Revelation that authorities noticed the unfortunate wording
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the analrapist joke even funnier by showing real-world consequences.

jokes.text: But since we have both started to grow hair in unexpected places, I supposed I shall soon be working as a leading man.
jokes.setup: Tobias talking about why he stopped his therapy practice
jokes.punchline: Bizarre connection between puberty and acting career prospects
jokes.explanation: Pure Tobias delusion - connecting random physical changes to acting opportunities. Character-specific absurdity.

jokes.text: I was helping he would be gifted sexually.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Maeby dating older boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Tobias's inappropriate and awkward phrasing about teen sexuality
jokes.explanation: Awkward Tobias moment that's uncomfortable but character-appropriate. Shows his cluelessness about appropriate boundaries.

jokes.text: So you're saying shred the evidence? / No, that's illegal... / So you're saying shred the evidence?
jokes.setup: Lawyer explaining legal constraints
jokes.punchline: Michael repeating the same illegal question despite clear answer
jokes.explanation: Good repetition joke showing Michael's selective hearing. Structure works well with the repeated question.

jokes.text: Wink. Did you say 'wink' or did you wink?
jokes.setup: Lawyer giving illegal advice while maintaining plausible deniability
jokes.punchline: Literally saying the word 'wink' instead of winking
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about communication. The literal interpretation of a gesture is absurdist but logical.

jokes.text: Steve Holt's not my son. / Steve Holt? With the moron jock? That's my son, you pothead.
jokes.setup: Gob denying parentage of Steve Holt
jokes.punchline: George Sr. revealing Steve Holt IS Gob's son through insults
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reveals important plot information through character conflict. George Sr.'s delivery style is perfect.

jokes.text: Wait, so that means that Steve Holt is my cousin? And Maeby's cousin, too.
jokes.setup: Revelation that Steve Holt is Gob's son
jokes.punchline: George Michael realizing the family connection implications
jokes.explanation: Sets up later comedy about cousin relationships. George Michael's innocent delivery makes it work.

jokes.text: At the kennel. / Right. Sorry. You lobbed that one right over the plate, Michael. Home run.
jokes.setup: Gob sarcastically suggesting Michael's date location
jokes.punchline: Gob congratulating himself for the obvious insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - being proud of an obvious insult. The baseball metaphor shows his need to explain his 'success'.

jokes.text: I've been having such fun on this space shuttle.
jokes.setup: Rita arriving at American-themed restaurant
jokes.punchline: Calling the restaurant booth a 'space shuttle' with genuine enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Subtle hint at Rita's condition through her childlike interpretation of themed restaurant decor.

jokes.text: I guess this is what the British think of Americans. / They like our food. / Whatever do you mean?
jokes.setup: Michael commenting on the over-the-top American restaurant
jokes.punchline: Rita completely missing the sarcasm about American food stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Another subtle hint at Rita's condition - she doesn't understand irony or cultural commentary.

jokes.text: your average American male is in perpetual state of adolescence, you know. arrested development. / Hey, that's the name of the show.
jokes.setup: Michael using psychology terms to describe American men
jokes.punchline: Narrator pointing out the show's title reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta moment that feels natural in context. The narrator's matter-of-fact delivery makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: If you like the small, why don't you come back for the medium?
jokes.setup: American-themed restaurant with over-the-top portions
jokes.punchline: Server suggesting even larger sizes after they've barely touched the 'small'
jokes.explanation: Standard commentary on American portion sizes. Serviceable but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: I don't have a family. / I can't believe I've never told anybody that before.
jokes.setup: Michael trapped by his earlier lie about not having family
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on the lie with apparent relief
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's desperation and self-deception. The relief is both funny and sad.

jokes.text: No, no, it's pronounced a-nal-ra-pist.
jokes.setup: Tobias offering his business card to Buster
jokes.punchline: Tobias still not understanding why his professional title is problematic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that shows Tobias learned nothing from previous problems. His pronunciation correction is perfectly oblivious.

jokes.text: It wasn't really the pronunciation that bothered me.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining the pronunciation of 'analrapist'
jokes.punchline: Buster's understated response about the real problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Buster's deadpan delivery and understatement make this land perfectly.

jokes.text: If this was a Lifetime Moment of Truth movie, this would be our act break.
jokes.setup: Emotional moment between Buster and Tobias about standing up to Lucille
jokes.punchline: Tobias reducing genuine emotion to TV movie structure
jokes.explanation: Great meta-humor that's perfectly Tobias - turning real emotion into entertainment analysis. Shows his emotional distance.

jokes.text: You want me to be explicit? / Yes, but I will be needing to get off in four minutes.
jokes.setup: Lindsay discussing unfulfilling marriage with lawyer
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'get off' - leaving vs. sexual climax
jokes.explanation: Solid double entendre that works in both legal and sexual contexts. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: Well, let's see if I can't hit that target for you.
jokes.setup: Bob Loblaw's unintended sexual innuendo about timing
jokes.punchline: Lindsay continuing the sexual metaphor with 'hitting the target'
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the double entendre. Lindsay's sexual aggression is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I thought the Donna Karan worked; you just didn't have the right shoes.
jokes.setup: Michael questioning Lucille's fashion choices for surrogate training
jokes.punchline: Lucille treating psychological surrogacy like a fashion problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - reducing complex human interaction to fashion coordination. Shows her superficiality.

jokes.text: Awesome! So you and I can party alone at your house.
jokes.setup: Maeby trying to cancel date by mentioning family emergency
jokes.punchline: Steve Holt seeing family emergency as romantic opportunity
jokes.explanation: Shows Steve Holt's single-minded focus on sex. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: she'd just green-lit two films that dealt with the very issue
jokes.setup: Maeby not knowing how to handle sexual pressure from Steve
jokes.punchline: As a movie executive, she's approved films about this exact situation
jokes.explanation: Clever irony about Maeby's adult job contrasting with her actual teenage inexperience.

jokes.text: Michael. I'm doing your cousin tonight. / So are you. / What? She's your cousin, too.
jokes.setup: George Michael announcing plans with 'cousin'
jokes.punchline: Revealing that Maeby is also Michael's family relation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays with family relationships. The confusion is well-structured.

jokes.text: No, he was just analraping me.
jokes.setup: Family asking why Buster isn't dressed for party
jokes.punchline: Buster using 'analrapist' as a verb innocently
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that turns the word into casual vocabulary. Buster's innocent delivery makes it perfect.

jokes.text: Well, in our defense, Mom, we're not exactly dog people.
jokes.setup: Lucille saying they haven't been great with Michael's girlfriends
jokes.punchline: Lindsay references Gob's earlier dog insults as if they were literal
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that treats the metaphorical insult as literal, showing Lindsay's obtuseness.

jokes.text: You know, we need some weather balloons for this party and maybe a tank of hydrogen. You know, really float those suckers.
jokes.setup: Discussion of party decorations
jokes.punchline: Gob suggesting dangerous hydrogen instead of safe helium for balloons
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for later explosion. Shows Gob's dangerous incompetence in character-appropriate way.

jokes.text: Hey, talk about cathartic. Now that's an act break.
jokes.setup: Buster overcoming fear by hitting Rita
jokes.punchline: Narrator commenting on the dramatic timing
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier meta-comment about TV structure. Narrator's dry delivery works well.

jokes.text: They're pills that create a sort of temporary forgettingness. So if somebody finds out how you do a trick, you just give 'em one of these, and they forget the whole thing. It's a mainstay of the magician's toolkit, like how clowns always have a rag soaked in ether.
jokes.setup: Family asking what Forget-Me-Nows are
jokes.punchline: Gob casually describing roofies as magic supplies, comparing to clown ether
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that perfectly captures Gob's moral blindness. The clown comparison escalates the absurdity beautifully.

jokes.text: They're pills that create a sort of temporary forgettingness. It's a mainstay of the magician's toolkit, like how clowns always have a rag soaked in ether.
jokes.setup: Explaining what Forget-Me-Now pills do
jokes.punchline: Casually comparing drugging people to clowns using ether
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic. Gob treats drugging people as normal professional equipment. The clown comparison is wonderfully dark.

jokes.text: Gob, this is Flunitrazepam. It's a roofie. Those are illegal. / Shut up, Mom. Don't make me give you another one of these.
jokes.setup: Lindsay pointing out that Forget-Me-Nows are date rape drugs
jokes.punchline: Gob threatening to drug his own mother
jokes.explanation: Escalates the roofie joke to even darker territory. Gob's casual family drugging is shocking but character-consistent.

jokes.text: Well, this sends a mixed message.
jokes.setup: Maeby has prepared a romantic setting while planning to reject Steve
jokes.punchline: Steve points out the obvious contradiction
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about mixed signals. Steve shows surprising awareness.

jokes.text: Take it down. We don't want anything in this house that says 'Rita.'
jokes.setup: Michael upset about missing Rita, wanting to remove party decorations
jokes.punchline: Ordering removal of 'Welcome Rita' banner while Rita is literally unconscious nearby
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows Rita is there while Michael doesn't. The timing creates tension and humor.

jokes.text: Take a look at banner, Michael!
jokes.setup: Family revealing the surprise party banner
jokes.punchline: The banner reading 'FAMILY LOVE MICHAEL' instead of proper grammar
jokes.explanation: Iconic visual gag. The grammatically incorrect banner became one of the show's most memorable images.

jokes.text: Everyone froze. Michael feared he'd been caught in a lie about his family. His family feared they'd been caught by a woman they clubbed, drugged and left on a bench.
jokes.setup: Rita appearing at the party unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Narrator explaining everyone's different but equally guilty fears
jokes.explanation: Perfect narration that captures the absurd parallel anxieties. Structure is excellent - building to the more serious crime.

jokes.text: Perhaps if the sergeant had spoken more slowly she would have.
jokes.setup: Revelation about Rita not understanding military ceremony
jokes.punchline: Narrator's subtle hint about Rita's mental condition affecting comprehension
jokes.explanation: Subtle revelation delivered perfectly by narrator. The understatement makes it funnier and more respectful.

jokes.text: Everybody's favorite son. / Mr. Terrible.
jokes.setup: Michael's status changing due to lying about family
jokes.punchline: Dramatic reversal from favorite to least favorite
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how quickly family dynamics shift. The economy of the reversal is perfect.

jokes.text: Buster, you should have finished the job. Job? What job? Did I say job? I meant Gob.
jokes.setup: Lucille saying Buster should have 'finished the job' (killing Rita)
jokes.punchline: Tries to cover by claiming she meant 'Gob' not 'job'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with clever wordplay. Lucille accidentally reveals her violent thoughts then tries to cover with word similarity.

jokes.text: It has caused me nothing but pride and self-respect!
jokes.setup: Buster wanting to get rid of his military medal
jokes.punchline: Complaining about positive emotions as if they're bad things
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster line - treating positive self-worth as a burden. Shows his psychology perfectly.

jokes.text: But it turns out that hydrogen is more explosive than helium.
jokes.setup: George Sr. trying to escape with hydrogen balloons
jokes.punchline: Narrator's matter-of-fact explanation of explosive results
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff delivered with narrator's signature understatement. Science lesson as punchline works well.

jokes.text: I gave him a roofie. A girl's got to grow up sometime.
jokes.setup: George Michael finding unconscious Steve Holt
jokes.punchline: Maeby treating drugging as coming-of-age milestone
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that shows Maeby learning terrible lessons from her family. The casual attitude is perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: Well, I think even the anti-drug people are going to be okay with that.
jokes.setup: Maeby explaining she drugged Steve to avoid sex
jokes.punchline: George Michael finding moral justification for date rape drug use
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Michael's innocent logic applied to dark situation. The moral reasoning is absurdist.

jokes.text: I'm willing to debase myself. / Get a maid's uniform and come by my house around 7:00. / There is no way to misinterpret that. / Except the one.
jokes.setup: Lindsay offering to work off debt to lawyer
jokes.punchline: Both characters misunderstanding the obviously sexual request as actual maid work
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke. The narrator's final observation adds the perfect punctuation.

jokes.text: I've made a huge mistake. / I know the feeling. I had you. / I'm your father, Steve Holt! I can't hide from it anymore.
jokes.setup: Steve Holt realizing he's been drugged
jokes.punchline: Gob admitting paternity while insulting his son
jokes.explanation: Good callback that resolves the paternity subplot. Gob's simultaneous insult and acceptance is character-perfect.

jokes.text: I won't forget this... Dad. / I will.
jokes.setup: Steve Holt's emotional response to father revelation
jokes.punchline: Gob planning to drug himself to forget the moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark ending that caps the episode's themes. Gob choosing to forget his own emotional breakthrough is tragically funny.

tmdb_overview: George Sr. is placed under house arrest by the family's new attorney Bob Loblaw, and finds that it's not exactly a walk in the park. Michael sets up his first date with Rita, but Rita has an unfortunate encounter with the family beforehand.
insight: Season 3's callback density peaks here with 24 nested references in 57 jokes.
